title: Teen arrested for stabbing death of Worcester man in Times Square
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime The 17-year-old is accused of two other stabbings, including one on this past Sunday, court documents show.
New York Police Department officers in Times Square in Manhattan, NYC.

Adam Gray / AFP via Getty Images Previously: A 17-year-old was arrested Thursday in connection with the fatal stabbing of a Worcester man in Times Square in New York City Monday night, authorities said.

title: Red Sox pitcher offers candid quote after latest setback

It sucks.

title: Fiancée of fallen Trooper Kevin Trainor shares heartfelt message
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News "You weren’t just a Hero to me, but a Hero to all.
" Jessica Ostrowski (left) and fallen Trooper Kevin Trainor (right), shared on Ostrowski's Instagram.

Courtesy of Jessica Ostrowski Kevin Trainor: The fiancée of Massachusetts State Trooper Kevin Trainor, who died in the line of duty responding to a wrong-way driver call on Route 1 in Lynnfield, has shared a heartfelt tribute.

title: ‘Senior assassin’ game in Stoneham leads police to briefly detain high schooler
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News The teenager is heard on the body camera footage explaining he was holding a "senior assassin water gun" and apologizing to the officer.
Body camera footage showing police approach a teenager who was holding what was later determined to be a water gun.
Stoneham police Stoneham police are warning high school students who are playing the game “senior assassin” after a concerned resident called 911 reporting a man with a gun outside his home.

title: The Bruins have a clear offseason goal. How they achieve it is the real question.

title: Lowell nonprofits provide essentials, housing after fire ravages 6 houses, displaces 40 residents
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News Fire officials explained that the flames spread quickly due to high winds, dry conditions, and close proximity between the homes.
The Lowell community is coming together to help victims of a devastating fire Tuesday.
Ken McGagh/The Boston Globe Local nonprofits are supporting the Lowell community after a devastating fire destroyed six homes and displaced 40 residents Tuesday.
